items.title: Function contracts and return values: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? If parameter and return types compile, all valid domain inputs and failure behavior are already defined by the signature.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Document and test semantic preconditions, ownership, effects, errors, and guarantees in addition to the C++ type signature.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Document and test semantic preconditions, ownership, effects, errors, and guarantees in addition to the C++ type signature.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

items.title: Function contracts and return values: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing CPP checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: If parameter and return types compile, all valid domain inputs and failure behavior are already defined by the signature.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'semantic preconditions'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. Document and test semantic preconditions, ownership, effects, errors, and guarantees in addition to the C++ type signature.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact CPP token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.

items.title: Function contracts and return values: executable application
items.prompt: Implement safe_square with the documented input contract -1000000 through 1000000, returning std::optional<long long>; print the square or invalid.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: explicit precondition validation and optional result

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises explicit precondition validation and optional result under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
